conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by reading the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. Their name is Alex, and they're 18 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand their current situation since they're 18 years old.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, studying A-levels, at college, university, or in an approved training program)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in college doing their A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 18 and in approved education, I need to confirm a few more details.

**When did Alex turn 20, or are they still under 20 years old?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, Alex is still 18 - they haven't turned 20 yet. They're under 20.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements:

**Does Alex live with you, or do they live somewhere else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 18 years old (under 20)
- Alex is in approved education (doing A-levels at college)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the eligibility criteria. Child Benefit can continue until Alex turns 20, as long as they remain in approved education or training.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive Child Benefit, but you may need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex stops their education or training, or starts working 24 hours or more a week, you'll need to inform the Child Benefit Office as this would affect your entitlement.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child receives qualifying benefits (e.g. UC, ESA) in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and still under 20. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex is currently in college doing their A-levels, which counts as approved education. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old child Alex. Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at college), lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. All eligibility criteria are met.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and still under 20. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16, but you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex is currently in college doing their A-levels, which counts as approved education. You live with Alex in the UK, which means you are responsible for them. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you meet all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old child Alex. Alex is under 20 and in approved education (A-levels at college), lives with the claimant in the UK, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. All eligibility criteria are met.
